article_id: Preamble
text_snippet: Chapter 1 Fundamental provisions

article_id: Section 1. The Constitution
text_snippet: Finland is a sovereign republic. The constitution of Finland is established in this constitutional act. The constitution shall guarantee the inviolability of human dignity and the freedom and rights o

article_id: Section 10. The right to privacy
text_snippet: Everyone's private life, honour and the sanctity of the home are guaranteed. More detailed provisions on the protection of personal data are laid down by an Act. The secrecy of correspondence, telepho

article_id: Section 100. Composition of the Supreme Court and the Supreme Administrative Court
text_snippet: The Supreme Court and the Supreme Administrative Court are composed of the President of the Court and the requisite number of Justices. The Supreme Court and the Supreme Administrative Court have a co

article_id: Section 101. High Court of Impeachment
text_snippet: The High Court of Impeachment deals with charges brought against a member of the Government, the Chancellor of Justice, the Parliamentary Ombudsman or a member of the Supreme Court or the Supreme Admi

article_id: Section 102. Appointment of judges
text_snippet: Tenured judges are appointed by the President of the Republic in accordance with the procedure laid down by an Act. Provisions on the appointment of other judges are laid down by an Act.

article_id: Section 103. The right of judges to remain in office
text_snippet: A judge shall not be suspended from office, except by a judgement of a court of law. In addition, a judge shall not be transferred to another office without his or her consent, except where the transf

article_id: Section 104. The prosecutors
text_snippet: The prosecution service is headed by the highest prosecutor, the Prosecutor-General, who is appointed by the President of the Republic. More detailed provisions on the prosecution service are laid dow

article_id: Section 105. Presidential pardon
text_snippet: In individual cases, the President of the Republic may, after obtaining a statement from the Supreme Court, grant full or partial pardon from a penalty or other criminal sanction imposed by a court of

article_id: Section 106. Primacy of the Constitution
text_snippet: If, in a matter being tried by a court of law, the application of an Act would be in evident conflict with the Constitution, the court of law shall give primacy to the provision in the Constitution.

article_id: Section 107. Subordination of lower-level statutes
text_snippet: If a provision in a Decree or another statute of a lower level than an Act is in conflict with the Constitution or another Act, it shall not be applied by a court of law or by any other public authori

article_id: Section 108. Duties of the Chancellor of Justice of the Government
text_snippet: The Chancellor of Justice shall oversee the lawfulness of the official acts of the Government and the President of the Republic. The Chancellor of Justice shall also ensure that the courts of law, the

article_id: Section 109. Duties of the Parliamentary Ombudsman
text_snippet: The Ombudsman shall ensure that the courts of law, the other authorities and civil servants, public employees and other persons, when the latter are performing a public task, obey the law and fulfill

article_id: Section 11. Freedom of religion and conscience
text_snippet: Everyone has the freedom of religion and conscience. Freedom of religion and conscience entails the right to profess and practice a religion, the right to express one's convictions and the right to be

article_id: Section 110. The right of the Chancellor of Justice and the Ombudsman to bring charges and the division of responsibilities between them
text_snippet: A decision to bring charges against a judge for unlawful conduct in office is made by the Chancellor of Justice or the Ombudsman. The Chancellor of Justice and the Ombudsman may prosecute or order tha

article_id: Section 111. The right of the Chancellor of Justice and Ombudsman to receive information
text_snippet: The Chancellor of Justice and the Ombudsman have the right to receive from public authorities or others performing public duties the information needed for their supervision of legality. The Chancello

article_id: Section 112. Supervision of the lawfulness of the official acts of the Government and the President of the Republic
text_snippet: If the Chancellor of Justice becomes aware that the lawfulness of a decision or measure taken by the Government, a Minister or the President of the Republic gives rise to a comment, the Chancellor sha

article_id: Section 113. Criminal liability of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: If the Chancellor of Justice, the Ombudsman or the Government deem that the President of the Republic is guilty of treason or high treason, or a crime against humanity, the matter shall be communicate

article_id: Section 114. Prosecution of Ministers
text_snippet: A charge against a Member of the Government for unlawful conduct in office is heard by the High Court of Impeachment, as provided in more detail by an Act. The decision to bring a charge is made by th

article_id: Section 115. Initiation of a matter concerning the legal responsibility of a Minister
text_snippet: An inquiry into the lawfulness of the official acts of a Minister may be initiated in the Constitutional Law Committee on the basis of: 1. A notification submitted to the Constitutional Law Committee

article_id: Section 116. Preconditions for the prosecution of a Minister
text_snippet: A decision to bring charges against a Member of the Government may be made if he or she has, intentionally or through gross negligence, essentially contravened his or her duties as a Minister or other

article_id: Section 117. Legal responsibility of the Chancellor of Justice and the Ombudsman
text_snippet: The provisions in sections 114 and 115 concerning a member of the Government apply to an inquiry into the lawfulness of the official acts of the Chancellor of Justice and the Ombudsman, the bringing o

article_id: Section 118. Official accountability
text_snippet: A civil servant is responsible for the lawfulness of his or her official actions. He or she is also responsible for a decision made by an official multi-member body that he or she has supported as one

article_id: Section 119. State administration
text_snippet: In addition to the Government and the Ministries, the central administration of the State may consist of agencies, institutions and other bodies. The State may also have regional and local public auth

article_id: Section 12. Freedom of expression and right of access to information
text_snippet: Everyone has the freedom of expression. Freedom of expression entails the right to express, disseminate and receive information, opinions and other communications without prior prevention by anyone. M

article_id: Section 120. Special Status of the Åland Islands
text_snippet: The Åland Islands have self-government in accordance with what is specifically stipulated in the Act on the Autonomy of the Åland Islands.

article_id: Section 121. Municipal and other regional self-government
text_snippet: Finland is divided into municipalities, whose administration shall be based on the self-government of their residents. Provisions on the general principles governing municipal administration and the d

article_id: Section 122. Administrative divisions
text_snippet: In the organisation of administration, the objective shall be suitable territorial divisions, so that the Finnish-speaking and Swedish-speaking populations have an opportunity to receive services in t

article_id: Section 123. Universities and other education providers
text_snippet: The universities are self-governing, as provided in more detail by an Act. Provisions on the principles governing the other educational services arranged by the State and the municipalities, as well a

article_id: Section 124. Delegation of administrative tasks to others than the authorities
text_snippet: A public administrative task may be delegated to others than public authorities only by an Act or by virtue of an Act, if this is necessary for the appropriate performance of the task and if basic rig

article_id: Section 125. General qualifications for public office and other grounds for appointment
text_snippet: It may be stated in an Act that only Finnish citizens are eligible for appointment to certain public offices or duties. The general qualifications for public office shall be skill, ability and proven

article_id: Section 126. Appointment to State offices (1112/2011, entry into force 1.3.2012)
text_snippet: The Government appoints state officials unless the appointment has been designated as a prerogative of the President of the Republic, a Ministry or another public authority. The President appoints the

article_id: Section 127. National defence obligation
text_snippet: Every Finnish citizen is obligated to participate or assist in national defence, as provided by an Act. Provisions on the right to exemption, on grounds of conscience, from participation in military n

article_id: Section 128. Commander-in-chief of the defence forces
text_snippet: The President of the Republic is the commander-in-chief of the defence forces. On the proposal of the Government in situations of emergency, the President may relinquish this task to another Finnish c

article_id: Section 129. Mobilisation
text_snippet: On the proposal of the Government, the President of the Republic decides on the mobilisation of the defence forces. If the Parliament is not in session at that moment, it shall be convened at once. Ch

article_id: Section 13. Freedom of assembly and freedom of association
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to arrange meetings and demonstrations without a permit, as well as the right to participate in them. Everyone has the freedom of association. Freedom of association entails the

article_id: Section 130. Entry into force
text_snippet: This Constitution shall enter into force on 1 March 2000. Detailed provisions necessary for the implementation of the Constitution are laid down by an Act.

article_id: Section 131. Repeal of Constitutional Acts
text_snippet: This Constitution repeals the following constitutional Acts, as amended: 1. The Constitution Act of Finland, of 17 July 1919; 2. The Parliament Act, of 13 January 1928; 3. The Act on the High Court of

article_id: Section 14. Electoral and participatory rights
text_snippet: Every Finnish citizen who has reached eighteen years of age has the right to vote in national elections and referendums. Specific provisions in this Constitution shall govern the eligibility to stand

article_id: Section 15. Protection of property
text_snippet: The property of everyone is protected. Provisions on the expropriation of property, for public needs and against full compensation, are laid down by an Act.

article_id: Section 16. Educational rights
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to basic education free of charge. Provisions on the duty to receive education are laid down by an Act. The public authorities shall, as provided in more detail by an Act, guara

article_id: Section 17. Right to one's language and culture
text_snippet: The national languages of Finland are Finnish and Swedish. The right of everyone to use his or her own language, either Finnish or Swedish, before courts of law and other authorities, and to receive o

article_id: Section 18. The right to work and the freedom to engage in commercial activity
text_snippet: Everyone has the right, as provided by an Act, to earn his or her livelihood by the employment, occupation or commercial activity of his or her choice. The public authorities shall take responsibility

article_id: Section 19. The right to social security
text_snippet: Those who cannot obtain the means necessary for a life of dignity have the right to receive indispensable subsistence and care. Everyone shall be guaranteed by an Act the right to basic subsistence in

article_id: Section 2. Democracy and the rule of law
text_snippet: The powers of the State in Finland are vested in the people, who are represented by the Parliament. Democracy entails the right of the individual to participate in and influence the development of soc

article_id: Section 20. Responsibility for the environment
text_snippet: Nature and its biodiversity, the environment and the national heritage are the responsibility of everyone. The public authorities shall endeavour to guarantee for everyone the right to a healthy envir

article_id: Section 21. Protection under the law
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to have his or her case dealt with appropriately and without undue delay by a legally competent court of law or other authority, as well as to have a decision pertaining to his

article_id: Section 22. Protection of basic rights and liberties
text_snippet: The public authorities shall guarantee the observance of basic rights and liberties and human rights.

article_id: Section 23. Basic rights and liberties in situations of emergency (1112/2011, entry into force 1.3.2012).
text_snippet: Such provisional exceptions to basic rights and liberties that are compatible with Finland's international human rights obligations and that are deemed necessary in the case of an armed attack against

article_id: Section 24. Composition and term of the Parliament
text_snippet: The Parliament is unicameral. It consists of two hundred Representatives, who are elected for a term of four years at a time. The term of the Parliament begins when the results of the parliamentary el

article_id: Section 25. Parliamentary elections
text_snippet: The Representatives shall be elected by a direct, proportional and secret vote. Every citizen who has the right to vote has equal suffrage in the elections. For the parliamentary elections, the countr

article_id: Section 26. Extraordinary parliamentary elections
text_snippet: The President of the Republic, in response to a reasoned proposal by the Prime Minister, and after having heard the parliamentary groups, and while the Parliament is in session, may order that extraor

article_id: Section 27. Eligibility and qualifications for the office of Representative
text_snippet: Everyone with the right to vote and who is not under guardianship can be a candidate in parliamentary elections. A person holding military office cannot, however, be elected as a Representative. The C

article_id: Section 28. Suspension of the office of a Representative and release or dismissal from office
text_snippet: The office of a Representative is suspended for the time during which the Representative is serving as a Member of the European Parliament. During that time a deputy of the Representative shall replac

article_id: Section 29. Independence of Representatives
text_snippet: A Representative is obliged to follow justice and truth in his or her office. He or she shall abide by the Constitution and no other orders are binding on him or her.

article_id: Section 3. Parliamentarism and the separation of powers
text_snippet: The legislative powers are exercised by the Parliament, which shall also decide on State finances. The governmental powers are exercised by the President of the Republic and the Government, the member

article_id: Section 30. Parliamentary immunity
text_snippet: A Representative shall not be prevented from carrying out his or her duties as a Representative. A Representative shall not be charged in a court of law nor be deprived of liberty owing to opinions ex

article_id: Section 31. Freedom of speech and conduct of Representatives
text_snippet: Each Representative has the right to speak freely in the Parliament on all matters under consideration and on how they are dealt with. A Representative shall conduct himself or herself with dignity an

article_id: Section 32. Conflict of interest
text_snippet: A Representative is disqualified from consideration of and decision-making in any matter that concerns him or her personally. However, he or she may participate in the debate on such matters in a plen

article_id: Section 33. Parliamentary session
text_snippet: The Parliament convenes in session every year at a time decided by the Parliament, after which the President of the Republic shall declare the parliamentary session open. The parliamentary session con

article_id: Section 34. The Speaker and the Speaker's Council
text_snippet: The Parliament elects from among its members a Speaker and two Deputy Speakers for each parliamentary session. The election of the Speaker and the Deputy Speakers is conducted by secret ballot. The Re

article_id: Section 35. Committees of the Parliament
text_snippet: For each electoral term, the Parliament appoints the Grand Committee, the Constitutional Law Committee, the Foreign Affairs Committee, the Finance Committee, the Audit Committee and the other standing

article_id: Section 36. Other bodies and delegates to be elected by the Parliament
text_snippet: The Parliament elects the trustees for monitoring the administration and operations of the Social Insurance Institution, as provided in more detail by an Act. The Parliament elects the other necessary

article_id: Section 37. Election of the parliamentary organs
text_snippet: The Committees and the other parliamentary organs are appointed during the first parliamentary session of an electoral term for the duration of that term, unless otherwise provided in this Constitutio

article_id: Section 38. Parliamentary Ombudsman
text_snippet: The Parliament appoints for a term of four years a Parliamentary Ombudsman and two Deputy Ombudsmen, who shall have outstanding knowledge of law. A Deputy Ombudsman may have a substitute as provided i

article_id: Section 39. How matters are initiated for consideration in the Parliament
text_snippet: Matters are initiated for consideration in the Parliament on the basis of a Government proposal or a motion submitted by the Government or a motion submitted by a Representative, or in another manner

article_id: Section 4. The Territory of Finland
text_snippet: The territory of Finland is indivisible. The national borders cannot be altered without the consent of the Parliament.

article_id: Section 40. Preparation of matters
text_snippet: Government proposals, motions by Representatives, reports submitted to the Parliament and other matters, as provided for in this Constitution or in the Parliament's Rules of Procedure, shall be prepar

article_id: Section 41. Consideration of matters in plenary session
text_snippet: A legislative proposal and a proposal on the Parliament's Rules of Procedure are considered in plenary session in two readings. However, a legislative proposal left in abeyance and an Act left unconfi

article_id: Section 42. Duties of the Speaker in a plenary session
text_snippet: The Speaker convenes the plenary sessions, presents the matters on the agenda, oversees the debate and ensures that the Constitution is complied with in the consideration of matters in plenary session

article_id: Section 43. Interpellations
text_snippet: A group of at least twenty Representatives may address an interpellation to the Government or to an individual Minister on a matter within the competence of the Government or the Minister. The interpe

article_id: Section 44. Statements and reports of the Government
text_snippet: The Government may present a statement or report to the Parliament on a matter relating to the governance of the country or its international relations. At the conclusion of the consideration of a sta

article_id: Section 45. Questions, announcements and debates
text_snippet: Each Representative has the right to address questions to a Minister on matters within the Minister's competence. Provisions on the questions and the answers are laid down in the Parliament's Rules of

article_id: Section 46. Reports to be submitted to the Parliament
text_snippet: The Government shall submit to the Parliament annual reports on governmental activities and on the measures undertaken in response to parliamentary decisions, as well as annual reports on State financ

article_id: Section 47. Parliamentary right to receive information
text_snippet: The Parliament has the right to receive from the Government the information it needs in the consideration of matters. The appropriate Minister shall ensure that Committees and other parliamentary orga

article_id: Section 48. Right of attendance of Ministers, the Ombudsman and the Chancellor of Justice
text_snippet: Minister has the right to attend and to participate in debates in plenary sessions of the Parliament even if the Minister is not a Representative. A Minister may not be a member of a Committee of the

article_id: Section 49. Continuity of consideration
text_snippet: Consideration of matters unfinished in one parliamentary session continues in the following parliamentary session, unless parliamentary elections have been held in the meantime. When necessary, the co

article_id: Section 5. Finnish citizenship
text_snippet: A child acquires Finnish citizenship at birth and through the citizenship of its parents, as provided in more detail by an Act. Citizenship may also be granted upon notification or application, subjec

article_id: Section 50. Public nature of parliamentary activity
text_snippet: The plenary sessions of the Parliament are open to the public, unless the Parliament for a very weighty reason decides otherwise for a given matter. The Parliament publishes its papers, as provided in

article_id: Section 51. Languages used in parliamentary work
text_snippet: The Finnish or Swedish languages are used in parliamentary work. The Government and the other authorities shall submit the documents necessary for a matter to be taken up for consideration in the Parl

article_id: Section 52. Parliament's Rules of Procedure and other instructions and rules of procedure
text_snippet: More detailed provisions on the procedures to be followed in the Parliament, as well as on parliamentary organs and parliamentary work are issued in the Parliament's Rules of Procedure. The Parliament

article_id: Section 53. Referendum and citizens’ initiative (new title, 1112/2011, entry into force 1.3.2012)
text_snippet: The decision to organise a consultative referendum is made by an Act, which shall contain provisions on the time of the referendum and on the choices to be presented to the voters. Provisions concerni

article_id: Section 54. Election of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of the Republic is elected by a direct vote for a term of six years. The President shall be a native-born Finnish citizen. The same person may be elected President for no more than two c

article_id: Section 55. The presidential term
text_snippet: The President of the Republic assumes office on the first day of the calendar month following his or her election into office. The term of the President ends when the President elected in the next ele

article_id: Section 56. Solemn affirmation of the President
text_snippet: When the President of the Republic assumes office, he or she shall make the following solemn affirmation before the Parliament: "I, , elected by the people of Finland as the President of the Republic,

article_id: Section 57. Duties of the President
text_snippet: The President of the Republic carries out the duties stated in this Constitution or specifically stated in another Act.

article_id: Section 58. Decisions of the President
text_snippet: The President of the Republic makes decisions in Government on the basis of motions proposed by the Government. If the President does not make the decision in accordance with the motion proposed by th

article_id: Section 59. Substitutes of the President
text_snippet: When the President of the Republic is prevented from carrying out of his or her duties, these are taken over by the Prime Minister or, if the Prime Minister too is incapacitated, by the Minister actin

article_id: Section 6. Equality
text_snippet: Everyone is equal before the law. No one shall, without an acceptable reason, be treated differently from other persons on the ground of sex, age, origin, language, religion, conviction, opinion, heal

article_id: Section 60. The Government
text_snippet: The Government consists of the Prime Minister and the necessary number of Ministers. The Ministers shall be Finnish citizens known to be honest and competent. The Ministers are responsible before the

article_id: Section 61. Formation of the Government
text_snippet: The Parliament elects the Prime Minister, who is thereafter appointed to the office by the President of the Republic. The President appoints the other Ministers in accordance with a proposal made by t

article_id: Section 62. Statement on the programme of the Government
text_snippet: The Government shall without delay submit its programme to the Parliament in the form of a statement. The same applies when the composition of the Government is essentially altered.

article_id: Section 63. Ministers' personal interests
text_snippet: While holding the office of a Minister, a member of the Government shall not hold any other public office or undertake any other task which may obstruct the performance of his or her ministerial dutie

article_id: Section 64. Resignation of the Government or a Minister
text_snippet: The President of the Republic grants, upon request, the resignation of the Government or a Minister. The President may also grant the resignation of a Minister on the proposal of the Prime Minister. T

article_id: Section 65. Duties of the Government
text_snippet: The Government has the duties specifically provided in this Constitution, as well as the other governmental and administrative duties which have been assigned to the Government or a Minister or which

article_id: Section 66. Duties of the Prime Minister
text_snippet: The Prime Minister directs the activities of the Government and oversees the preparation and consideration of matters that come within the mandate of the Government. The Prime Minister chairs the plen

article_id: Section 67. Decision-making in the Government
text_snippet: The matters within the authority of the Government are decided at the plenary meetings of the Government or at the Ministry to which the matter belongs. Matters of wide importance or matters that are

article_id: Section 68. The Ministries
text_snippet: The Government has the requisite number of Ministries. Each Ministry, within its proper purview, is responsible for the preparation of matters to be considered by the Government and for the appropriat

article_id: Section 69. The Chancellor of Justice of the Government
text_snippet: Attached to the Government, there is a Chancellor of Justice and a Deputy Chancellor of Justice, who are appointed by the President of the Republic, and who shall have outstanding knowledge of law. In

article_id: Section 7. The right to life, personal liberty and integrity
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to life, personal liberty, integrity and security. No one shall be sentenced to death, tortured or otherwise treated in a manner violating human dignity. The personal integrity

article_id: Section 70. Legislative initiative
text_snippet: The proposal for the enactment of an Act is initiated in the Parliament through a government proposal submitted by the Government or through a legislative motion submitted by a Representative. Legisla

article_id: Section 71. Supplementation and withdrawal of a government proposal
text_snippet: A government proposal may be supplemented through a new complementary proposal or it may be withdrawn. A complementary proposal cannot be submitted once the Committee preparing the matter has issued i

article_id: Section 72. Consideration of a legislative proposal in the Parliament
text_snippet: Once the relevant report of the Committee preparing the matter has been issued, a legislative proposal is considered in two readings in a plenary session of the Parliament. In the first reading of the

article_id: Section 73. Procedure for constitutional enactment
text_snippet: A proposal on the enactment, amendment or repeal of the Constitution or on the enactment of a limited derogation of the Constitution shall in the second reading be left in abeyance, by a majority of t

article_id: Section 74. Supervision of constitutionality
text_snippet: The Constitutional Law Committee shall issue statements on the constitutionality of legislative proposals and other matters brought for its consideration, as well as on their relation to international

article_id: Section 75. Special legislation for the Åland Islands
text_snippet: The legislative procedure for the Act on the Autonomy of the Åland Islands and the Act on the Right to Acquire Real Estate in the Åland Islands is governed by the specific provisions in those Acts. Th

article_id: Section 76. The Church Act
text_snippet: Provisions on the organisation and administration of the Evangelic Lutheran Church are laid down in the Church Act. The legislative procedure for enactment of the Church Act and the right to submit le

article_id: Section 77. Confirmation of Acts
text_snippet: An Act adopted by the Parliament shall be submitted to the President of the Republic for confirmation. The President shall decide on the confirmation within three months of the submission of the Act.

article_id: Section 78. Consideration of an unconfirmed Act
text_snippet: If the President of the Republic has not confirmed an Act within the time provided, it shall without delay be taken up for reconsideration in the Parliament. Once the pertinent report of the Committee

article_id: Section 79. Publication and entry into force of Acts
text_snippet: If an Act has been enacted in accordance with the procedure for constitutional enactment, this is indicated in the Act. An Act which has been confirmed or which enters into force without confirmation

article_id: Section 8. The principle of legality in criminal cases
text_snippet: No one shall be found guilty of a criminal offence or be sentenced to a punishment on the basis of a deed, which has not been determined punishable by an Act at the time of its commission. The penalty

article_id: Section 80. Issuance of Decrees and delegation of legislative powers
text_snippet: The President of the Republic, the Government and a Ministry may issue Decrees on the basis of authorisation given to them in this Constitution or in another Act. However, the principles governing the

article_id: Section 81. State taxes and charges
text_snippet: The state tax is governed by an Act, which shall contain provisions on the grounds for tax liability and the amount of the tax, as well as on the legal remedies available to the persons or entities li

article_id: Section 82. State debt and guarantees
text_snippet: The incurrence of State debt shall be based on the consent of the Parliament, which indicates the maximum level of new debt or the total level of State debt. A State security and a State guarantee may

article_id: Section 83. State budget
text_snippet: The Parliament decides on the State budget for one budgetary year at a time. It is published in the Statute Book of Finland. The government proposal concerning the State budget and the other proposals

article_id: Section 84. Contents of the budget
text_snippet: Estimates of the annual revenues and appropriations for the annual expenditures of the State, the reasons for the appropriations and other justifications of the budget shall be included in the State b

article_id: Section 85. Appropriations in the budget
text_snippet: The appropriations are taken up in the budget as fixed appropriations, estimated appropriations or transferable appropriations. An estimated appropriation may be exceeded and a transferable appropriat

article_id: Section 86. Supplementary budget
text_snippet: A proposal of the Government for a supplementary budget shall be submitted to the Parliament, if there is a justified reason for amending the budget. A Representative may submit budgetary motions for

article_id: Section 87. Extra-budgetary funds
text_snippet: An extra-budgetary fund may be created by an Act, if the performance of a permanent duty of the State requires this in an essential manner. However, the decision of the Parliament to adopt a legislati

article_id: Section 88. Legitimate receivables from the State to private parties
text_snippet: Regardless of the budget, everyone has the right to collect his or her legitimate receivables from the State.

article_id: Section 89. Approval of the terms of service of State officials and employees
text_snippet: The appropriate Committee of the Parliament accepts, in the name of the Parliament, agreements on the terms of service of State officials and employees, in so far as this requires the consent of the P

article_id: Section 9. Freedom of movement
text_snippet: Finnish citizens and foreigners legally resident in Finland have the right to freely move within the country and to choose their place of residence. Everyone has the right to leave the country. Limita

article_id: Section 90. Supervision and audit of State finances
text_snippet: The Parliament supervises State finances and compliance with the State budget. For this purpose, the Parliament shall have an Audit Committee. The Audit Committee shall report any significant supervis

article_id: Section 91. The Bank of Finland
text_snippet: The Bank of Finland operates under the guarantee and supervision of the Parliament, as provided by an Act. For the purpose of supervising the operations of the Bank of Finland, the Parliament elects i

article_id: Section 92. State assets
text_snippet: Provisions on the competence and procedure in the use of shareholder authority in companies effectively controlled by the State are laid down by an Act. Provisions on the necessity for the consent of

article_id: Section 93. Competence in the area of foreign policy issues
text_snippet: The foreign policy of Finland is directed by the President of the Republic in co-operation with the Government. However, the Parliament accepts Finland's international obligations and their denounceme

article_id: Section 94. Acceptance of international obligations and their denouncement
text_snippet: The acceptance of the Parliament is required for such treaties and other international obligations that contain provisions of a legislative nature, are otherwise significant, or otherwise require appr

article_id: Section 95. Bringing into force of international obligations
text_snippet: The provisions of treaties and other international obligations, in so far as they are of a legislative nature, are brought into force by an Act. Otherwise, international obligations are brought into f

article_id: Section 96. Participation of the Parliament in the national preparation of European Union matters
text_snippet: The Parliament considers those proposals for acts, agreements and other measures which are to be decided in the European Union and which otherwise, according to the Constitution, would fall within the

article_id: Section 97. Parliamentary right to receive information on international affairs
text_snippet: The Foreign Affairs Committee of the Parliament shall receive from the Government, upon request and when otherwise necessary, reports of matters pertaining to foreign and security policy. Correspondin

article_id: Section 98. Courts of law
text_snippet: The Supreme Court, the Courts of Appeal and the District Courts are the general courts of law. The Supreme Administrative Court and the regional Administrative Courts are the general courts of adminis

article_id: Section 99. Duties of the Supreme Court and the Supreme Administrative Court
text_snippet: Justice in civil, commercial and criminal matters is in the final instance administered by the Supreme Court. Justice in administrative matters is in the final instance administered by the Supreme Adm
